title: Multi-arch image build is pushing too many tags
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

The multi-arch image build is updating the most current versioned tag, as well as the "latest" tag. According to the README, once a versioned tag is cut, it should not be updated. Currently though, the v3.2.3 tag is being updated every time the latest tag is built.
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. No manual steps. The automation is doing the push. Just have to look at quay.io/podman/stable, and see that latest and v3.2.3 are the same.

**Describe the results you received:**

v3.2.3 keeps getting updated.

**Describe the results you expected:**

v3.2.3 should not be updated.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

From the README.md in contrib/podmanimage:

quay.io/containers/podman:<version> and quay.io/podman/stable:<version> - These images are built when a new Podman version becomes available in Fedora. These images are intended to be unchanging and stable, they will never be updated by automation once they've been pushed. For build details, please see the configuration file.

comments.body: Maybe the documentation needs to be updated.  But these images are not static, they will get updated with the latest software in Fedora in order to maintain their security.  What won't be updated is the version of Podman within the container.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I think this can be closed - "Fixed" in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11190  Users wanting stable images (for now) can reference them by tag + sha.

For the long-term, I'm re-architecting the automation that builds these (for many reasons).  Once that's in place and working, there will be an open window to more easily add "intelligence" into the process.  For example, to avoid pushing entirely new images unless a security-related update is included.  Or similar things like this.
